mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.9% in June 2021 reflects post-pandemic labor recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market tightening
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer demand and economic activity, signaling sustained corporate revenue growth that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 620,214M in May 2021 reflects strong consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 60d returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances despite strong retail sales growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) begins accelerating above 2.5% year-over-year, real rates turn negative, compressing equity risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities as inflation hedges.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% and CPIAUCSL at 270.981 in June 2021
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with emerging inflation pressures
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 60% of instances when both conditions are met simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side constraints that compress profit margins and reduce forward earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 100.0951 in June 2021 shows near-trend production levels
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 20d returns in fewer than 52% of instances when INDPRO declines exceed 0.3% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly signal broad-based economic expansion and construction sector strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as both reflect sustained capital investment and labor demand.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1500K units AND PAYEMS growth > 0.2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1572K and PAYEMS at 145,759K in May-June 2021
regime_hint: synchronized expansion across housing and employment
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 60d returns in fewer than 62% of instances when both conditions are jointly satisfied.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) exceeding 0.4% month-over-month signals tight labor markets and rising wage pressures that trigger inflation expectations, causing the Fed to signal tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 145,759K in June 2021 reflects strong post-pandemic hiring
regime_hint: tight labor market with inflation acceleration risk
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.015 over any 1-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 5d returns in fewer than 50% of instances when PAYEMS growth exceeds 0.4% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below 2% annualized signals economic slowdown that forces the Fed to maintain accommodative policy, supporting equity valuations through lower discount rates and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19,086.375B in Q1 2021 reflects post-pandemic recovery trajectory
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 60d returns in fewer than 56% of instances when GDPC1 growth falls below 2.0% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3% year-over-year while real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month, real consumer purchasing power erodes and discretionary spending weakens, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings growth expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 3.0% AND RSAFS MoM growth < 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 270.981 and RSAFS at 620,214M in May-June 2021
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 20d returns in fewer than 54% of instances when both inflation and retail sales conditions are jointly triggered.
